Adani Defence & Aerospace acquires General Aeronautics in UAV push

ET Bureau · Updated: 25 June 2026

Adani Defence said on Wednesday it has acquired a majority stake in Bengaluru-based General Aeronautics for about ₹200 cr, expanding its unmanned aerial systems portfolio. The acquisition gives Adani Aerospace access to agricultural and tactical drone technology.

The company, part of the Adani Group, has been scaling its defence business and recently showcased the Drishti-10 Starliner MALE UAV. Analysts said the General Aeronautics deal deepens Adani's drone capabilities against rivals.

"This is a strategic addition to our UAV roadmap," an Adani Defence spokesperson said. The transaction is expected to close by the end of the quarter.
